NKJV.1: The proverbs of Solomon: A wise son makes a glad father, But a foolish son is the grief of his mother.
NKJV.2: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing,  But righteousness delivers from death.
NKJV.3: The LORD will not allow the righteous soul to famish, But He casts away the desire of the wicked.
NKJV.4: He who has a slack hand becomes poor, But the hand of the diligent makes rich.
NKJV.5: He who gathers in summer is a wise son; He who sleeps in harvest is a son who causes shame.
NKJV.6: Blessings are on the head of the righteous, But violence covers the mouth of the wicked.
NKJV.7: The memory of the righteous is blessed, But the name of the wicked will rot.
NKJV.8: The wise in heart will receive commands,  But a prating fool will fall.
NKJV.9: He who walks with integrity walks securely, But he who perverts his ways will become known.
NKJV.10: He who winks with the eye causes trouble, But a prating fool will fall.
NKJV.11: The mouth of the righteous is a well of life, But violence covers the mouth of the wicked.
NKJV.12: Hatred stirs up strife, But love covers all sins.
NKJV.13: Wisdom is found on the lips of him who has understanding, But a rod is for the back of him who is devoid of understanding.
NKJV.14: Wise people store up knowledge, But the mouth of the foolish is near destruction.
NKJV.15: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city; The destruction of the poor is their poverty.
NKJV.16: The labor of the righteous leads to life, The wages of the wicked to sin.
NKJV.17: He who keeps instruction is in the way of life, But he who refuses correction goes astray.
NKJV.18: Whoever hides hatred has lying lips, And whoever spreads slander is a fool.
NKJV.19: In the multitude of words sin is not lacking, But he who restrains his lips is wise.
NKJV.20: The tongue of the righteous is choice silver; The heart of the wicked is worth little.
NKJV.21: The lips of the righteous feed many, But fools die for lack of wisdom.
NKJV.22: The blessing of the LORD makes one rich, And He adds no sorrow with it.
NKJV.23: To do evil is like sport to a fool, But a man of understanding has wisdom.
NKJV.24: The fear of the wicked will come upon him, And the desire of the righteous will be granted.
NKJV.25: When the whirlwind passes by, the wicked is no more, But the righteous has an everlasting foundation.
NKJV.26: As vinegar to the teeth and smoke to the eyes, So is the lazy man to those who send him.
NKJV.27: The fear of the LORD prolongs days, But the years of the wicked will be shortened.
NKJV.28: The hope of the righteous will be gladness, But the expectation of the wicked will perish.
NKJV.29: The way of the LORD is strength for the upright, But destruction will come to the workers of iniquity.
NKJV.30: The righteous will never be removed, But the wicked will not inhabit the earth.
NKJV.31: The mouth of the righteous brings forth wisdom, But the perverse tongue will be cut out.
NKJV.32: The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable, But the mouth of the wicked what is perverse.

ESV.1: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son makes a glad father, but a foolish son is a sorrow to his mother.
ESV.2: Treasures gained by wickedness do not profit, but righteousness delivers from death.
ESV.3: The LORD does not let the righteous go hungry, but he thwarts the craving of the wicked.
ESV.4: A slack hand causes poverty, but the hand of the diligent makes rich.
ESV.5: He who gathers in summer is a prudent son, but he who sleeps in harvest is a son who brings shame.
ESV.6: Blessings are on the head of the righteous, but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
ESV.7: The memory of the righteous is a blessing, but the name of the wicked will rot.
ESV.8: The wise of heart will receive commandments, but a babbling fool will come to ruin.
ESV.9: Whoever walks in integrity walks securely, but he who makes his ways crooked will be found out.
ESV.10: Whoever winks the eye causes trouble, and a babbling fool will come to ruin.
ESV.11: The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life, but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
ESV.12: Hatred stirs up strife, but love covers all offenses.
ESV.13: On the lips of him who has understanding, wisdom is found, but a rod is for the back of him who lacks sense.
ESV.14: The wise lay up knowledge, but the mouth of a fool brings ruin near.
ESV.15: A rich man's wealth is his strong city; the poverty of the poor is their ruin.
ESV.16: The wage of the righteous leads to life, the gain of the wicked to sin.
ESV.17: Whoever heeds instruction is on the path to life, but he who rejects reproof leads others astray.
ESV.18: The one who conceals hatred has lying lips, and whoever utters slander is a fool.
ESV.19: When words are many, transgression is not lacking, but whoever restrains his lips is prudent.
ESV.20: The tongue of the righteous is choice silver; the heart of the wicked is of little worth.
ESV.21: The lips of the righteous feed many, but fools die for lack of sense.
ESV.22: The blessing of the LORD makes rich, and he adds no sorrow with it.
ESV.23: Doing wrong is like a joke to a fool, but wisdom is pleasure to a man of understanding.
ESV.24: What the wicked dreads will come upon him, but the desire of the righteous will be granted.
ESV.25: When the tempest passes, the wicked is no more, but the righteous is established forever.
ESV.26: Like vinegar to the teeth and smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to those who send him.
ESV.27: The fear of the LORD prolongs life, but the years of the wicked will be short.
ESV.28: The hope of the righteous brings joy, but the expectation of the wicked will perish.
ESV.29: The way of the LORD is a stronghold to the blameless, but destruction to evildoers.
ESV.30: The righteous will never be removed, but the wicked will not dwell in the land.
ESV.31: The mouth of the righteous brings forth wisdom, but the perverse tongue will be cut off.
ESV.32: The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable, but the mouth of the wicked, what is perverse.

NASB.1: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son makes a father glad, But a foolish son is a grief to his mother.
NASB.2: Ill-gotten gains do not profit, But righteousness delivers from death.
NASB.3: The LORD will not allow the righteous to hunger, But He will reject the craving of the wicked.
NASB.4: Poor is he who works with a negligent hand, But the hand of the diligent makes rich.
NASB.5: He who gathers in summer is a son who acts wisely, [But] he who sleeps in harvest is a son who acts shamefully.
NASB.6: Blessings are on the head of the righteous, But the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
NASB.7: The memory of the righteous is blessed, But the name of the wicked will rot.
NASB.8: The wise of heart will receive commands, But a babbling fool will be ruined.
NASB.9: He who walks in integrity walks securely, But he who perverts his ways will be found out.
NASB.10: He who winks the eye causes trouble, And a babbling fool will be ruined.
NASB.11: The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life, But the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
NASB.12: Hatred stirs up strife, But love covers all transgressions.
NASB.13: On the lips of the discerning, wisdom is found, But a rod is for the back of him who lacks understanding.
NASB.14: Wise men store up knowledge, But with the mouth of the foolish, ruin is at hand.
NASB.15: The rich man's wealth is his fortress, The ruin of the poor is their poverty.
NASB.16: The wages of the righteous is life, The income of the wicked, punishment.
NASB.17: He is [on] the path of life who heeds instruction, But he who ignores reproof goes astray.
NASB.18: He who conceals hatred [has] lying lips, And he who spreads slander is a fool.
NASB.19: When there are many words, transgression is unavoidable, But he who restrains his lips is wise.
NASB.20: The tongue of the righteous is [as] choice silver, The heart of the wicked is [worth] little.
NASB.21: The lips of the righteous feed many, But fools die for lack of understanding.
NASB.22: It is the blessing of the LORD that makes rich, And He adds no sorrow to it.
NASB.23: Doing wickedness is like sport to a fool, And [so is] wisdom to a man of understanding.
NASB.24: What the wicked fears will come upon him, But the desire of the righteous will be granted.
NASB.25: When the whirlwind passes, the wicked is no more, But the righteous [has] an everlasting foundation.
NASB.26: Like vinegar to the teeth and smoke to the eyes, So is the lazy one to those who send him.
NASB.27: The fear of the LORD prolongs life, But the years of the wicked will be shortened.
NASB.28: The hope of the righteous is gladness, But the expectation of the wicked perishes.
NASB.29: The way of the LORD is a stronghold to the upright, But ruin to the workers of iniquity.
NASB.30: The righteous will never be shaken, But the wicked will not dwell in the land.
NASB.31: The mouth of the righteous flows with wisdom, But the perverted tongue will be cut out.
NASB.32: The lips of the righteous bring forth what is acceptable, But the mouth of the wicked what is perverted.

NLT.1:
The proverbs of Solomon: A wise child brings joy to a father;
a foolish child brings grief to a mother.
NLT.2:
Tainted wealth has no lasting value,
but right living can save your life.
NLT.3:
The LORD will not let the godly go hungry,
but he refuses to satisfy the craving of the wicked.
NLT.4:
Lazy people are soon poor;
hard workers get rich.
NLT.5:
A wise youth harvests in the summer,
but one who sleeps during harvest is a disgrace.
NLT.6:
The godly are showered with blessings;
the words of the wicked conceal violent intentions.
NLT.7:
We have happy memories of the godly,
but the name of a wicked person rots away.
NLT.8:
The wise are glad to be instructed,
but babbling fools fall flat on their faces.
NLT.9:
People with integrity walk safely,
but those who follow crooked paths will be exposed.
NLT.10:
People who wink at wrong cause trouble,
but a bold reproof promotes peace.
NLT.11:
The words of the godly are a life-giving fountain;
the words of the wicked conceal violent intentions.
NLT.12:
Hatred stirs up quarrels,
but love makes up for all offenses.
NLT.13:
Wise words come from the lips of people with understanding,
but those lacking sense will be beaten with a rod.
NLT.14:
Wise people treasure knowledge,
but the babbling of a fool invites disaster.
NLT.15:
The wealth of the rich is their fortress;
the poverty of the poor is their destruction.
NLT.16:
The earnings of the godly enhance their lives,
but evil people squander their money on sin.
NLT.17:
People who accept discipline are on the pathway to life,
but those who ignore correction will go astray.
NLT.18:
Hiding hatred makes you a liar;
slandering others makes you a fool.
NLT.19:
Too much talk leads to sin.
Be sensible and keep your mouth shut.
NLT.20:
The words of the godly are like sterling silver;
the heart of a fool is worthless.
NLT.21:
The words of the godly encourage many,
but fools are destroyed by their lack of common sense.
NLT.22:
The blessing of the LORD makes a person rich,
and he adds no sorrow with it.
NLT.23:
Doing wrong is fun for a fool,
but living wisely brings pleasure to the sensible.
NLT.24:
The fears of the wicked will be fulfilled;
the hopes of the godly will be granted.
NLT.25:
When the storms of life come, the wicked are whirled away,
but the godly have a lasting foundation.
NLT.26:
Lazy people irritate their employers,
like vinegar to the teeth or smoke in the eyes.
NLT.27:
Fear of the LORD lengthens one’s life,
but the years of the wicked are cut short.
NLT.28:
The hopes of the godly result in happiness,
but the expectations of the wicked come to nothing.
NLT.29:
The way of the LORD is a stronghold to those with integrity,
but it destroys the wicked.
NLT.30:
The godly will never be disturbed,
but the wicked will be removed from the land.
NLT.31:
The mouth of the godly person gives wise advice,
but the tongue that deceives will be cut off.
NLT.32:
The lips of the godly speak helpful words,
but the mouth of the wicked speaks perverse words.

WEB.1: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son makes a glad father; but a foolish son brings grief to his mother.
WEB.2: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing, but righteousness delivers from death.
WEB.3: Yahweh will not allow the soul of the righteous to go hungry, but he thrusts away the desire of the wicked.
WEB.4: He becomes poor who works with a lazy hand, but the hand of the diligent brings wealth.
WEB.5: He who gathers in summer is a wise son, but he who sleeps during the harvest is a son who causes shame.
WEB.6: Blessings are on the head of the righteous, but violence covers the mouth of the wicked.
WEB.7: The memory of the righteous is blessed, but the name of the wicked will rot.
WEB.8: The wise in heart accept commandments, but a chattering fool will fall.
WEB.9: He who walks blamelessly walks surely, but he who perverts his ways will be found out.
WEB.10: One winking with the eye causes sorrow, but a chattering fool will fall.
WEB.11: The mouth of the righteous is a spring of life, but violence covers the mouth of the wicked.
WEB.12: Hatred stirs up strife, but love covers all wrongs.
WEB.13: Wisdom is found on the lips of him who has discernment, but a rod is for the back of him who is void of understanding.
WEB.14: Wise men lay up knowledge, but the mouth of the foolish is near ruin.
WEB.15: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city. The destruction of the poor is their poverty.
WEB.16: The labor of the righteous leads to life. The increase of the wicked leads to sin.
WEB.17: He is in the way of life who heeds correction, but he who forsakes reproof leads others astray.
WEB.18: He who hides hatred has lying lips. He who utters a slander is a fool.
WEB.19: In the multitude of words there is no lack of disobedience, but he who restrains his lips does wisely.
WEB.20: The tongue of the righteous is like choice silver. The heart of the wicked is of little worth.
WEB.21: The lips of the righteous feed many, but the foolish die for lack of understanding.
WEB.22: Yahweh’s blessing brings wealth, and he adds no trouble to it.
WEB.23: It is a fool’s pleasure to do wickedness, but wisdom is a man of understanding’s pleasure.
WEB.24: What the wicked fear, will overtake them, but the desire of the righteous will be granted.
WEB.25: When the whirlwind passes, the wicked is no more; but the righteous stand firm forever.
WEB.26: As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to those who send him.
WEB.27: The fear of Yahweh prolongs days, but the years of the wicked shall be shortened.
WEB.28: The prospect of the righteous is joy, but the hope of the wicked will perish.
WEB.29: The way of Yahweh is a stronghold to the upright, but it is a destruction to the workers of iniquity.
WEB.30: The righteous will never be removed, but the wicked will not dwell in the land.
WEB.31: The mouth of the righteous produces wisdom, but the perverse tongue will be cut off.
WEB.32: The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable, but the mouth of the wicked is perverse.

CSB17.1:
Solomon’s proverbs: ⓚ A wise son brings joy to his father,
but a foolish son, heartache to his mother. ⓛ
CSB17.2:
Ill-gotten gains do not profit anyone,
but righteousness rescues from death. ⓜ
CSB17.3:
The LORD will not let the righteous go hungry, ⓝ
but he denies the wicked what they crave. ⓐ
CSB17.4:
Idle hands make one poor,
but diligent hands bring riches. ⓑ
CSB17.5:
The son who gathers during summer is prudent;
the son who sleeps during harvest is disgraceful. ⓒ
CSB17.6:
Blessings are on the head of the righteous,
but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence. ⓓ
CSB17.7:
The remembrance of the righteous is a blessing,
but the name of the wicked will rot. ⓔ
CSB17.8:
A wise heart accepts commands, ⓕ
but foolish lips will be destroyed.
CSB17.9:
The one who lives with integrity lives securely, ⓖ
but whoever perverts his ways will be found out. ⓗ
CSB17.10:
A sly wink of the eye ⓘ causes grief,
and foolish lips will be destroyed.
CSB17.11:
The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life, ⓙ
but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence. ⓚ
CSB17.12:
Hatred stirs up conflicts,
but love covers all offenses. ⓛ
CSB17.13:
Wisdom is found on the lips of the discerning,
but a rod is for the back of the one who lacks sense. ⓜ
CSB17.14:
The wise store up knowledge,
but the mouth of the fool hastens destruction. ⓝ
CSB17.15:
The wealth of the rich is his fortified city; ⓐ
the poverty of the poor is their destruction.
CSB17.16:
The reward of the righteous is life;
the wages of the wicked is punishment. ⓑ
CSB17.17:
The one who follows instruction is on the path to life,
but the one who rejects correction goes astray. ⓒ
CSB17.18:
The one who conceals hatred has lying lips,
and whoever spreads slander is a fool. ⓓ
CSB17.19:
When there are many words, sin is unavoidable,
but the one who controls his lips is prudent. ⓔ
CSB17.20:
The tongue of the righteous is pure silver;
the heart of the wicked is of little value. ⓕ
CSB17.21:
The lips of the righteous feed many,
but fools die for lack of sense. ⓖ
CSB17.22:
The LORD’s blessing enriches, ⓗ
and he adds no painful effort to it. [1] ⓘ
CSB17.23:
As shameful conduct is pleasure for a fool, ⓙ
so wisdom is for a person of understanding.
CSB17.24:
What the wicked dreads will come to him, ⓚ
but what the righteous desire will be given to them. ⓛ
CSB17.25:
When the whirlwind passes,
the wicked are no more, ⓜ
but the righteous are secure forever. ⓝ
CSB17.26:
Like vinegar to the teeth and smoke to the eyes, ⓐ
so the slacker is to the one who sends him on an errand.
CSB17.27:
The fear of the LORD prolongs life, [2] ⓑ
but the years of the wicked are cut short. ⓒ
CSB17.28:
The hope of the righteous is joy, ⓓ
but the expectation of the wicked will perish. ⓔ
CSB17.29:
The way of the LORD is a stronghold for the honorable,
but destruction awaits the malicious. ⓕ
CSB17.30:
The righteous will never be shaken,
but the wicked will not remain on the earth. ⓖ
CSB17.31:
The mouth of the righteous produces wisdom, ⓗ
but a perverse tongue will be cut out. ⓘ
CSB17.32:
The lips of the righteous know what is appropriate, ⓙ
but the mouth of the wicked, only what is perverse. ⓚ

AMP.1:
The proverbs of Solomon:
A wise son makes a father glad,
But a foolish [stubborn] son [who refuses to learn] is a grief to his mother.
AMP.2:
Treasures of wickedness and ill-gotten gains do not profit,
But righteousness and moral integrity in daily life rescues from death.
AMP.3:
The LORD will not allow the righteous to hunger [God will meet all his needs],
But He will reject and cast away the craving of the wicked.
AMP.4:
Poor is he who works with a negligent and idle hand,
But the hand of the diligent makes him rich.
AMP.5:
He who gathers during summer and takes advantage of his opportunities is a son who acts wisely,
But he who sleeps during harvest and ignores the moment of opportunity is a son who acts shamefully.
AMP.6:
Blessings are on the head of the righteous [the upright, those in right standing with God],
But the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
AMP.7:
The memory of the righteous [person] is a [source of] blessing,
But the name of the wicked will [be forgotten and] rot [like a corpse].
AMP.8:
The wise in heart [are willing to learn so they] will accept and obey commands (instruction),
But the babbling fool [who is arrogant and thinks himself wise] will come to ruin.
AMP.9:
He who walks in integrity and with moral character walks securely,
But he who takes a crooked way will be discovered and punished.
AMP.10:
He who [maliciously] winks the eye [of evil intent] causes trouble;
And the babbling fool [who is arrogant and thinks himself wise] will come to ruin.
AMP.11:
The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life and his words of wisdom are a source of blessing,
But the mouth of the wicked conceals violence and evil.
AMP.12:
Hatred stirs up strife,
But love covers and overwhelms all transgressions [forgiving and overlooking another's faults].
AMP.13:
On the lips of the discerning, [skillful and godly] wisdom is found,
But discipline and the rod are for the back of the one who is without common sense and understanding.
AMP.14:
Wise men store up and treasure knowledge [in mind and heart],
But with the mouth of the foolish, ruin is at hand.
AMP.15:
The rich man's wealth is his fortress;
The ruin of the poor is their poverty.
AMP.16:
The wages of the righteous [the upright, those in right standing with God] is [a worthwhile, meaningful] life,
The income of the wicked, punishment.
AMP.17:
He who learns from instruction and correction is on the [right] path of life [and for others his example is a path toward wisdom and blessing],
But he who ignores and refuses correction goes off course [and for others his example is a path toward sin and ruin].
AMP.18:
He who hides hatred has lying lips,
And he who spreads slander is a fool.
AMP.19:
When there are many words, transgression and offense are unavoidable,
But he who controls his lips and keeps thoughtful silence is wise.
AMP.20:
The tongue of the righteous is like precious silver (greatly valued);
The heart of the wicked is worth little.
AMP.21:
The lips of the righteous feed and guide many,
But fools [who reject God and His wisdom] die for lack of understanding.
AMP.22:
The blessing of the LORD brings [true] riches,
And He adds no sorrow to it [for it comes as a blessing from God].
AMP.23:
Engaging in evil is like sport to the fool [who refuses wisdom and chases sin],
But to a man of understanding [skillful and godly] wisdom brings joy.
AMP.24:
What the wicked fears will come upon him,
But the desire of the righteous [for the blessings of God] will be granted.
AMP.25:
When the whirlwind passes, the wicked is no more,
But the righteous has an everlasting foundation.
AMP.26:
Like vinegar to the teeth and smoke to the eyes,
So is the lazy one to those who send him to work.
AMP.27:
The [reverent] fear of the LORD [worshiping, obeying, serving, and trusting Him with awe-filled respect] prolongs one's life,
But the years of the wicked will be shortened.
AMP.28:
The hope of the righteous [those of honorable character and integrity] is joy,
But the expectation of the wicked [those who oppose God and ignore His wisdom] comes to nothing.
AMP.29:
The way of the LORD is a stronghold to the upright,
But it is ruin to those who do evil.
AMP.30:
The [consistently] righteous will never be shaken,
But the wicked will not inhabit the earth.
AMP.31:
The mouth of the righteous flows with [skillful and godly] wisdom,
But the perverted tongue will be cut out.
AMP.32:
The lips of the righteous know (speak) what is acceptable,
But the mouth of the wicked knows (speaks) what is perverted (twisted).

MSG.1:
Wise son, glad father;
stupid son, sad mother.
MSG.2:
Ill-gotten gain gets you nowhere;
an honest life is immortal.
MSG.3:
God won't starve an honest soul,
but he frustrates the appetites of the wicked.
MSG.4:
Sloth makes you poor;
diligence brings wealth.
MSG.5:
Make hay while the sun shines—that's smart;
go fishing during harvest—that's stupid.
MSG.6:
Blessings accrue on a good and honest life,
but the mouth of the wicked is a dark cave of abuse.
MSG.7:
A good and honest life is a blessed memorial;
a wicked life leaves a rotten stench.
MSG.8:
A wise heart takes orders;
an empty head will come unglued.
MSG.9:
Honesty lives confident and carefree,
but Shifty is sure to be exposed.
MSG.10:
An evasive eye is a sign of trouble ahead,
but an open, face-to-face meeting results in peace.
MSG.11:
The mouth of a good person is a deep, life-giving well,
but the mouth of the wicked is a dark cave of abuse.
MSG.12:
Hatred starts fights,
but love pulls a quilt over the bickering.
MSG.13:
You'll find wisdom on the lips of a person of insight,
but the shortsighted needs a slap in the face.
MSG.14:
The wise accumulate knowledge—a true treasure;
know-it-alls talk too much—a sheer waste.
MSG.15:
The wealth of the rich is their bastion;
the poverty of the indigent is their ruin.
MSG.16:
The wage of a good person is exuberant life;
an evil person ends up with nothing but sin.
MSG.17:
The road to life is a disciplined life;
ignore correction and you're lost for good.
MSG.18:
Liars secretly hoard hatred;
fools openly spread slander.
MSG.19:
The more talk, the less truth;
the wise measure their words.
MSG.20:
The speech of a good person is worth waiting for;
the blabber of the wicked is worthless.
MSG.21:
The talk of a good person is rich fare for many,
but chatterboxes die of an empty heart.
MSG.22:
God's blessing makes life rich;
nothing we do can improve on God.
MSG.23:
An empty-head thinks mischief is fun,
but a mindful person relishes wisdom.
MSG.24:
The nightmares of the wicked come true;
what the good people desire, they get.
MSG.25:
When the storm is over, there's nothing left of the wicked;
good people, firm on their rock foundation, aren't even fazed.
MSG.26:
A lazy employee will give you nothing but trouble;
it's vinegar in the mouth, smoke in the eyes.
MSG.27:
The Fear-of-God expands your life;
a wicked life is a puny life.
MSG.28:
The aspirations of good people end in celebration;
the ambitions of bad people crash.
MSG.29:
God is solid backing to a well-lived life,
but he calls into question a shabby performance.
MSG.30:
Good people last—they can't be moved;
the wicked are here today, gone tomorrow.
MSG.31:
A good person's mouth is a clear fountain of wisdom;
a foul mouth is a stagnant swamp.
MSG.32:
The speech of a good person clears the air;
the words of the wicked pollute it.

NIV.1:
Proverbs of Solomon
The proverbs of Solomon:
A wise son brings joy to his father,
but a foolish son grief to his mother.
NIV.2:
Ill-gotten treasures are of no value,
but righteousness delivers from death.
NIV.3:
The Lord does not let the righteous go hungry
but he thwarts the craving of the wicked.
NIV.4:
Lazy hands make a man poor,
but diligent hands bring wealth.
NIV.5:
He who gathers crops in summer is a wise son,
but he who sleeps during harvest is a disgraceful son.
NIV.6:
Blessings crown the head of the righteous,
but violence overwhelms the mouth of the wicked.
NIV.7:
The memory of the righteous will be a blessing,
but the name of the wicked will rot.
NIV.8:
The wise in heart accept commands,
but a chattering fool comes to ruin.
NIV.9:
The man of integrity walks securely,
but he who takes crooked paths will be found out.
NIV.10:
He who winks maliciously causes grief,
and a chattering fool comes to ruin.
NIV.11:
The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life,
but violence overwhelms the mouth of the wicked.
NIV.12:
Hatred stirs up dissension,
but love covers over all wrongs.
NIV.13:
Wisdom is found on the lips of the discerning,
but a rod is for the back of him who lacks judgment.
NIV.14:
Wise men store up knowledge,
but the mouth of a fool invites ruin.
NIV.15:
The wealth of the rich is their fortified city,
but poverty is the ruin of the poor.
NIV.16:
The wages of the righteous bring them life,
but the income of the wicked brings them punishment.
NIV.17:
He who heeds discipline shows the way to life,
but whoever ignores correction leads others astray.
NIV.18:
He who conceals his hatred has lying lips,
and whoever spreads slander is a fool.
NIV.19:
When words are many, sin is not absent,
but he who holds his tongue is wise.
NIV.20:
The tongue of the righteous is choice silver,
but the heart of the wicked is of little value.
NIV.21:
The lips of the righteous nourish many,
but fools die for lack of judgment.
NIV.22:
The blessing of the Lord brings wealth,
and he adds no trouble to it.
NIV.23:
A fool finds pleasure in evil conduct,
but a man of understanding delights in wisdom.
NIV.24:
What the wicked dreads will overtake him;
what the righteous desire will be granted.
NIV.25:
When the storm has swept by, the wicked are gone,
but the righteous stand firm forever.
NIV.26:
As vinegar to the teeth and smoke to the eyes,
so is a sluggard to those who send him.
NIV.27:
The fear of the Lord adds length to life,
but the years of the wicked are cut short.
NIV.28:
The prospect of the righteous is joy,
but the hopes of the wicked come to nothing.
NIV.29:
The way of the Lord is a refuge for the righteous,
but it is the ruin of those who do evil.
NIV.30:
The righteous will never be uprooted,
but the wicked will not remain in the land.
NIV.31:
The mouth of the righteous brings forth wisdom,
but a perverse tongue will be cut out.
NIV.32:
The lips of the righteous know what is fitting,
but the mouth of the wicked only what is perverse.

NRSVCE.1:
The proverbs of Solomon. A wise child makes a glad father,
but a foolish child is a mother’s grief.
NRSVCE.2:
Treasures gained by wickedness do not profit,
but righteousness delivers from death.
NRSVCE.3:
The LORD does not let the righteous go hungry,
but he thwarts the craving of the wicked.
NRSVCE.4:
A slack hand causes poverty,
but the hand of the diligent makes rich.
NRSVCE.5:
A child who gathers in summer is prudent,
but a child who sleeps in harvest brings shame.
NRSVCE.6:
Blessings are on the head of the righteous,
but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
NRSVCE.7:
The memory of the righteous is a blessing,
but the name of the wicked will rot.
NRSVCE.8:
The wise of heart will heed commandments,
but a babbling fool will come to ruin.
NRSVCE.9:
Whoever walks in integrity walks securely,
but whoever follows perverse ways will be found out.
NRSVCE.10:
Whoever winks the eye causes trouble,
but the one who rebukes boldly makes peace.
NRSVCE.11:
The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life,
but the mouth of the wicked conceals violence.
NRSVCE.12:
Hatred stirs up strife,
but love covers all offenses.
NRSVCE.13:
On the lips of one who has understanding wisdom is found,
but a rod is for the back of one who lacks sense.
NRSVCE.14:
The wise lay up knowledge,
but the babbling of a fool brings ruin near.
NRSVCE.15:
The wealth of the rich is their fortress;
the poverty of the poor is their ruin.
NRSVCE.16:
The wage of the righteous leads to life,
the gain of the wicked to sin.
NRSVCE.17:
Whoever heeds instruction is on the path to life,
but one who rejects a rebuke goes astray.
NRSVCE.18:
Lying lips conceal hatred,
and whoever utters slander is a fool.
NRSVCE.19:
When words are many, transgression is not lacking,
but the prudent are restrained in speech.
NRSVCE.20:
The tongue of the righteous is choice silver;
the mind of the wicked is of little worth.
NRSVCE.21:
The lips of the righteous feed many,
but fools die for lack of sense.
NRSVCE.22:
The blessing of the LORD makes rich,
and he adds no sorrow with it.
NRSVCE.23:
Doing wrong is like sport to a fool,
but wise conduct is pleasure to a person of understanding.
NRSVCE.24:
What the wicked dread will come upon them,
but the desire of the righteous will be granted.
NRSVCE.25:
When the tempest passes, the wicked are no more,
but the righteous are established forever.
NRSVCE.26:
Like vinegar to the teeth, and smoke to the eyes,
so are the lazy to their employers.
NRSVCE.27:
The fear of the LORD prolongs life,
but the years of the wicked will be short.
NRSVCE.28:
The hope of the righteous ends in gladness,
but the expectation of the wicked comes to nothing.
NRSVCE.29:
The way of the LORD is a stronghold for the upright,
but destruction for evildoers.
NRSVCE.30:
The righteous will never be removed,
but the wicked will not remain in the land.
NRSVCE.31:
The mouth of the righteous brings forth wisdom,
but the perverse tongue will be cut off.
NRSVCE.32:
The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable,
but the mouth of the wicked what is perverse.

NET.1: The Proverbs of Solomon: A wise child makes a father rejoice, but a foolish child is a grief to his mother.
NET.2: Treasures gained by wickedness do not profit, but righteousness delivers from mortal danger.
NET.3: The LORD satisfies the appetite of the righteous, but he thwarts the craving of the wicked.
NET.4: The one who is lazy becomes poor, but the one who works diligently becomes wealthy.
NET.5: The one who gathers crops in the summer is a wise son, but the one who sleeps during the harvest is a son who brings shame to himself.
NET.6: Blessings are on the head of the righteous, but the speech of the wicked conceals violence.
NET.7: The memory of the righteous is a blessing, but the reputation of the wicked will rot.
NET.8: The wise person accepts instructions, but the one who speaks foolishness will come to ruin.
NET.9: The one who conducts himself in integrity will live securely, but the one who behaves perversely will be found out.
NET.10: The one who winks his eye causes trouble, and the one who speaks foolishness will come to ruin.
NET.11: The teaching of the righteous is a fountain of life, but the speech of the wicked conceals violence.
NET.12: Hatred stirs up dissension, but love covers all transgressions.
NET.13: Wisdom is found in the words of the discerning person, but the one who lacks wisdom will be disciplined.
NET.14: Those who are wise store up knowledge, but foolish speech leads to imminent destruction.
NET.15: The wealth of a rich person is like a fortified city, but the poor are brought to ruin by their poverty.
NET.16: The reward which the righteous receive is life; the recompense which the wicked receive is judgment.
NET.17: The one who heeds instruction is on the way to life, but the one who rejects rebuke goes astray.
NET.18: The one who conceals hatred utters lies, and the one who spreads slander is certainly a fool.
NET.19: When words abound, transgression is inevitable, but the one who restrains his words is wise.
NET.20: What the righteous say is like the best silver, but what the wicked think is of little value.
NET.21: The teaching of the righteous feeds many, but fools die for lack of wisdom.
NET.22: The blessing from the LORD makes a person rich, and he adds no sorrow to it.
NET.23: Carrying out a wicked scheme is enjoyable to a fool, and so is wisdom for the one who has discernment.
NET.24: What the wicked fears will come on him; what the righteous desire will be granted.
NET.25: When the storm passes through, the wicked are swept away, but the righteous are an everlasting foundation.
NET.26: Like vinegar to the teeth and like smoke to the eyes, so is the sluggard to those who send him.
NET.27: Fearing the LORD prolongs life, but the life span of the wicked will be shortened.
NET.28: The hope of the righteous is joy, but the expectation of the wicked will remain unfulfilled.
NET.29: The way of the LORD is like a stronghold for the upright, but it is destruction to evildoers.
NET.30: The righteous will never be moved, but the wicked will not inhabit the land.
NET.31: The speech of the righteous bears the fruit of wisdom, but the one who speaks perversion will be destroyed.
NET.32: The lips of the righteous know what is pleasing, but the speech of the wicked is perverse.
